coherence_checks: Same-tag consistency: 'non-attachment-to-praise', 'team-culture', 'operator-system-coupling' — these align with 2.35 (which shares the latter two tags). The non-attachment-to-praise tag is appropriate because the verse is precisely about not letting reputation-grasping drive duty-abandonment, while still acknowledging the structural reality of peer-modelling.
Cross-reference resolution: Chapter 5 (right-renunciation) and 18.66 (closing release) referenced as correctives. Both pointers correct.
Cross-reference support: the citing claim (the verse names structural reputation-as-signal, not vanity) is supported by the broader Gītā discussion of niṣkāma-karma in 2.47-2.50.
Consistency with neighbours: 2.33 named the kīrti-loss; 2.34 develops it; 2.35 sharpens it. Three-step escalation is internally consistent.
verdict_reason: Doctrine coherent across tags and cross-references. PASS on both 6.1 and 6.5.
